target.name: Hermeus

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Supersonic & Hypersonic (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Supersonic & Hypersonic, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Hermeus, a Series C frontier-tech company in the Supersonic & Hypersonic sector, is valued at $1.0B post-money and has raised $350M to date. The comparable universe is sparse: two public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and one private peer (Boom Supersonic) operate in related domains, though narrow-filter peer matching yielded insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints for reliable median extraction.

The valuation analysis applies stage-progression methodology, the only defensible triangulation approach given peer scarcity. Using Hermeus's $1.0B last post-money valuation as the base and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage progression yields an implied value of $1.7B. This method is applicable because forward-stage step-ups are calibrated against historical venture outcomes and require no peer-dependent assumptions.

The analysis supports a median valuation of $1.7B, with a range of $1.4B to $2.0B reflecting uncertainty bounds around the stage-progression multiplier. The critical limitation is the absence of sufficiently dense peer data in the Supersonic & Hypersonic segment; reliance on broader-sector comparables risks contaminating the result. Key uncertainties include the appropriate step-up multiple for deep-tech aerospace companies and the trajectory of Hermeus's technical and commercial milestones relative to founding-stage risk.
